mechanism_narrative: Monthly industrial production growth predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns because expanding manufacturing output signals higher corporate revenues and capacity utilization.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or monthly INDPRO increases fail to coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating household income and consumer demand, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE has risen more than 0.3pp over the prior three months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds trailing twelve-month CPI inflation by more than 1.5 percentage points, restrictive real monetary policy generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds YoY CPIAUCSL inflation by > 1.5pp
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL signal fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a pooled regression.

mechanism_narrative: During housing-led slowdowns, declines in residential construction starts transmit to broader demand weakness and reduce corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10% below its trailing 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts peaked in 2006 and declined through 2007
failure_condition: Negative HOUST momentum fails to coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of housing-led slowdown episodes, or the signal underperforms a naive short strategy.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in nonfarm payrolls predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns because labor income expansion underpins consumption and aggregate demand.
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS increases fail to associate with positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% increases the cost of capital and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of declining industrial production and rising unemployment creates a joint recessionary signal that generates sharply negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines MoM while UNRATE rises MoM
failure_condition: The joint INDPRO-UNRATE signal fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered months, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns because aggregate output growth maps directly to nominal revenue growth and earnings surprises.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth fails to coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of quarters, or walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03.
